context_packet.task: Project setup and verification audit for GNN-PCNA. Use the following local documents as context: C:\Users\reshw\Downloads\AUDIT_2026-05-25.md and C:\Users\reshw\Downloads\DOCUMENTS_GNN\BORRA_#1_SARC.pdf. The project aim from BORRA_#1_SARC.pdf is: identify cryptic/allosteric binding sites on human PCNA using a residue-level GNN/GAT trained on allosteric/cryptic pocket labels, require recovery of the AOH1996 pocket in PDB 8GLA as a positive control, then validate prioritized sites using MD simulations with RMSF, pocket volume/shape, and DCCM. The audit identifies critical V1 problems: canonical source missing from main, virtual-node batch leak across proteins, optional symmetry prior batch leak, val/test leakage in combined_val_test reporting, stale ANM JSON values, PocketMiner baseline not run on same split, seed not applied, no seed sweep, no clean split protocol, stale claims/results, chain_id one-hot breaks PCNA homotrimer equivariance, unsafe/incomplete dataset/schema checks, and documentation drift. Task: produce an actionable project setup blueprint for phase 2 that tells us exactly how to set up the repo, data registry, split protocol, model code, training/evaluation protocol, MD validation, claims/provenance governance, and agent-based verification workflow. Emphasize how to avoid data leakage, computational errors, stale artifacts, overclaiming, and biological errors. Include required ResearchOS gates/agents and pass/fail criteria.

claim_registry.text: PocketGNNXL V3 achieves AUROC 0.8081 on 13 held-out CryptoSite proteins (8 val + 5 test), with AUPRC 0.3441 (6.2× above trivial baseline 0.0557).
disallowed_wording: validated on all proteins; AUROC without specifying N=13
claim_registry.notes: Primary generalization metric. Fine-tuning structures NOT included.
claim_registry.claim_text: PocketGNNXL V3 achieves AUROC 0.8081 on 13 held-out CryptoSite proteins (8 val + 5 test), with AUPRC 0.3441 (6.2× above trivial baseline 0.0557).

_legacy.text: PocketGNNXL V3 achieves AUROC 0.8081 on 13 held-out CryptoSite proteins (8 val + 5 test), with AUPRC 0.3441 (6.2× above trivial baseline 0.0557).
_legacy.disallowed_wording: validated on all proteins; AUROC without specifying N=13
_legacy.notes: Primary generalization metric. Fine-tuning structures NOT included.

claim_registry.text: PocketGNNXL V3 recovers the AOH1996 binding site on 8GLA with mean pocket score 0.8676 (threshold 0.700 → PASS). Recovers ≥20/24 ground-truth residues in 23/59 PCNA structures.
allowed_wording: recovers AOH1996 binding site; mean score 0.8676
disallowed_wording: discovers novel pocket at AOH1996 site (it is the ground truth, not a novel finding)
claim_registry.notes: Ground truth: 24 residues within 6 Å of ZQZ in 8GLA chains A+B only.
claim_registry.claim_text: PocketGNNXL V3 recovers the AOH1996 binding site on 8GLA with mean pocket score 0.8676 (threshold 0.700 → PASS). Recovers ≥20/24 ground-truth residues in 23/59 PCNA structures.

_legacy.text: PocketGNNXL V3 recovers the AOH1996 binding site on 8GLA with mean pocket score 0.8676 (threshold 0.700 → PASS). Recovers ≥20/24 ground-truth residues in 23/59 PCNA structures.
_legacy.allowed_wording: recovers AOH1996 binding site; mean score 0.8676
_legacy.disallowed_wording: discovers novel pocket at AOH1996 site (it is the ground truth, not a novel finding)
_legacy.notes: Ground truth: 24 residues within 6 Å of ZQZ in 8GLA chains A+B only.

claim_registry.text: ANM analysis shows the holo PCNA pocket (8GLA) has elevated flexibility vs global (fold-change 1.157, delta +0.300 vs apo 0.857); holo internal DCCM 0.2093 vs apo 0.0995.
allowed_wording: elevated ANM flexibility in holo pocket; fold-change delta +0.300
disallowed_wording: MD-validated pocket opening; confirmed dynamic pocket (requires trajectory evidence)
claim_registry.notes: ANM is normal-mode approximation, not MD. Supports but does not prove dynamic pocket.
claim_registry.claim_text: ANM analysis shows the holo PCNA pocket (8GLA) has elevated flexibility vs global (fold-change 1.157, delta +0.300 vs apo 0.857); holo internal DCCM 0.2093 vs apo 0.0995.

_legacy.text: ANM analysis shows the holo PCNA pocket (8GLA) has elevated flexibility vs global (fold-change 1.157, delta +0.300 vs apo 0.857); holo internal DCCM 0.2093 vs apo 0.0995.
_legacy.allowed_wording: elevated ANM flexibility in holo pocket; fold-change delta +0.300
_legacy.disallowed_wording: MD-validated pocket opening; confirmed dynamic pocket (requires trajectory evidence)
_legacy.notes: ANM is normal-mode approximation, not MD. Supports but does not prove dynamic pocket.

claim_registry.text: PocketGNNXL V3 (+ESM2) outperforms PocketGNN V1 by +0.228 AUROC on 7 internal fine-tuning structures (0.9067 vs 0.6782).
disallowed_wording: generalization improvement (these are fine-tuning structures, not held-out)
claim_registry.notes: ⚠️ Table 1 data only. Always label as internal, not generalization.
claim_registry.claim_text: PocketGNNXL V3 (+ESM2) outperforms PocketGNN V1 by +0.228 AUROC on 7 internal fine-tuning structures (0.9067 vs 0.6782).

_legacy.text: PocketGNNXL V3 (+ESM2) outperforms PocketGNN V1 by +0.228 AUROC on 7 internal fine-tuning structures (0.9067 vs 0.6782).
_legacy.disallowed_wording: generalization improvement (these are fine-tuning structures, not held-out)
_legacy.notes: ⚠️ Table 1 data only. Always label as internal, not generalization.

claim_registry.text: The AOH1996 binding pocket on PCNA is a cryptic site: invisible in apo structure but exhibiting elevated flexibility in the ligand-bound form by ANM.
allowed_wording: candidate cryptic site; suggestive of dynamic accessibility
disallowed_wording: validated cryptic pocket; confirmed pocket opening
claim_registry.notes: Upgrade to moderately_supported requires MD RMSF + volume evidence. Pending E003.
claim_registry.claim_text: The AOH1996 binding pocket on PCNA is a cryptic site: invisible in apo structure but exhibiting elevated flexibility in the ligand-bound form by ANM.

_legacy.text: The AOH1996 binding pocket on PCNA is a cryptic site: invisible in apo structure but exhibiting elevated flexibility in the ligand-bound form by ANM.
_legacy.allowed_wording: candidate cryptic site; suggestive of dynamic accessibility
_legacy.disallowed_wording: validated cryptic pocket; confirmed pocket opening
_legacy.notes: Upgrade to moderately_supported requires MD RMSF + volume evidence. Pending E003.

experiment_registry.name: Baseline GNN + Full Training
experiment_registry.purpose: Train PocketGNN V1 and PocketGNNXL V3; evaluate on held-out CryptoSite and PCNA structures
experiment_registry.hypothesis: GATv2Conv + ESM2 will outperform baseline GATv2Conv on held-out cryptic pocket detection
experiment_registry.expected_outcome: V3 AUROC > 0.80 on held-out; AOH1996 gate PASS
experiment_registry.actual_outcome: V3 held-out AUROC 0.8081; AOH gate 0.8676 PASS

_legacy.name: Baseline GNN + Full Training
_legacy.purpose: Train PocketGNN V1 and PocketGNNXL V3; evaluate on held-out CryptoSite and PCNA structures
_legacy.hypothesis: GATv2Conv + ESM2 will outperform baseline GATv2Conv on held-out cryptic pocket detection

_legacy.actual_outcome: V3 held-out AUROC 0.8081; AOH gate 0.8676 PASS

experiment_registry.title: Baseline GNN + Full Training
experiment_registry.hypothesis_tested: GATv2Conv + ESM2 will outperform baseline GATv2Conv on held-out cryptic pocket detection

experiment_registry.purpose: Compare ANM flexibility of predicted pocket in apo (1W60) vs holo (8GLA) PCNA
experiment_registry.hypothesis: Holo pocket will show elevated fold-change and DCCM vs apo

_legacy.purpose: Compare ANM flexibility of predicted pocket in apo (1W60) vs holo (8GLA) PCNA
_legacy.hypothesis: Holo pocket will show elevated fold-change and DCCM vs apo

experiment_registry.title: ANM Structural Dynamics
experiment_registry.hypothesis_tested: Holo pocket will show elevated fold-change and DCCM vs apo

experiment_registry.name: MD Validation — 100 ns OpenMM
experiment_registry.purpose: Confirm cryptic pocket opens dynamically in apo 1W60 via RMSF + pocket volume tracking
experiment_registry.hypothesis: Predicted pocket residues will show RMSF > 1.5 Å and transient volume > 100 Å³
experiment_registry.notes: Running on Google Cloud L4. DCD: data/md/1W60_production.dcd. ~140 ns/day, 100 ns likely done.

_legacy.name: MD Validation — 100 ns OpenMM
_legacy.purpose: Confirm cryptic pocket opens dynamically in apo 1W60 via RMSF + pocket volume tracking
_legacy.hypothesis: Predicted pocket residues will show RMSF > 1.5 Å and transient volume > 100 Å³
_legacy.notes: Running on Google Cloud L4. DCD: data/md/1W60_production.dcd. ~140 ns/day, 100 ns likely done.

experiment_registry.title: MD Validation — 100 ns OpenMM
experiment_registry.hypothesis_tested: Predicted pocket residues will show RMSF > 1.5 Å and transient volume > 100 Å³

issue_registry.title: 9B8T chain assignment wrong
issue_registry.description: Chain A in 9B8T is DNA polymerase epsilon, not PCNA. PCNA = chains B/C/D. All 9B8T outputs are biologically incorrect.
issue_registry.fix_required: Re-run inference on 9B8T with chains B/C/D
issue_registry.notes: Do not cite any 9B8T result until regenerated.

_legacy.title: 9B8T chain assignment wrong
_legacy.description: Chain A in 9B8T is DNA polymerase epsilon, not PCNA. PCNA = chains B/C/D. All 9B8T outputs are biologically incorrect.
_legacy.fix_required: Re-run inference on 9B8T with chains B/C/D
_legacy.notes: Do not cite any 9B8T result until regenerated.

issue_registry.description: Old holo=1.104, delta=+0.247. Correct: holo=1.157, delta=+0.300. Any pre-fix output file is stale.
stale_artifacts: any ANM report or figure before 2026-05-19
issue_registry.notes: Main repo and paper are already corrected.

_legacy.description: Old holo=1.104, delta=+0.247. Correct: holo=1.157, delta=+0.300. Any pre-fix output file is stale.

issue_registry.title: LIMITATIONS.md line 26 claimed 8GLA absent from all training
issue_registry.description: Wrong: 8GLA IS in PCNA fine-tuning. Absent from held-out CryptoSite eval only.
issue_registry.notes: External shared copies may still carry wrong claim.

_legacy.title: LIMITATIONS.md line 26 claimed 8GLA absent from all training
_legacy.description: Wrong: 8GLA IS in PCNA fine-tuning. Absent from held-out CryptoSite eval only.
_legacy.notes: External shared copies may still carry wrong claim.

issue_registry.title: T6 sanity test fails (chain_id one-hot equivariance)
issue_registry.description: 6/7 model sanity tests pass. T6 fails because chain_id one-hot breaks permutation equivariance.
issue_registry.fix_required: Document as limitation, or remove chain_id feature

_legacy.title: T6 sanity test fails (chain_id one-hot equivariance)
_legacy.description: 6/7 model sanity tests pass. T6 fails because chain_id one-hot breaks permutation equivariance.
_legacy.fix_required: Document as limitation, or remove chain_id feature

actions: Run agents in order: context_source_truth, research_design, dataset_integrity, leakage_split, compute_planning, visual_evidence, reviewer_collaboration, model_training, validation_skeptic, biological_realism, contradiction_hunter, document_knowledge_ingestion, paper_claim, metrics_statistics, provenance_artifacts, preprocessing_auditor, scientific_code_review, testing_environment
Evaluate gates: claim, evaluation, validation, dataset, leakage, preprocessing, code
Block paper writing if claim/validation/leakage gates are not pass.
Reject metrics if dataset, leakage, preprocessing, or code gates fail.
Require explicit MD evidence classification before any validation claim.
Run contradiction hunter at end of pipeline.

agent_outputs.summary: Reviewer simulation: 12 canonical questions, 10 open risks tracked.

reviewer_questions: How did you prevent homology leakage?
Where is AUPRC and what is the positive-class baseline?
Did MD actually validate the prediction, or only simulation stability?
Were old checkpoints regenerated after bug fixes?
Can the repo run from scratch?
Is the test set independent at the protein level (not residue level)?
Were baselines compared under the same leakage-clean split?
open_reviewer_risks: How many independent test proteins exist?
Were baselines compared under the same split?
Does MD actually validate the exact claim?
Were old checkpoints regenerated after bug fixes?
Can the repo run from scratch?

agent_outputs.task: Project setup and verification audit for GNN-PCNA. Use the following local documents as context: C:\Users\reshw\Downloads\AUDIT_2026-05-25.md and C:\Users\reshw\Downloads\DOCUMENTS_GNN\BORRA_#1_SARC.pdf. The project aim from BORRA_#1_SARC.pdf is: identify cryptic/allosteric binding sites on human PCNA using a residue-level GNN/GAT trained on allosteric/cryptic pocket labels, require recovery of the AOH1996 pocket in PDB 8GLA as a positive control, then validate prioritized sites using MD simulations with RMSF, pocket volume/shape, and DCCM. The audit identifies critical V1 problems: canonical source missing from main, virtual-node batch leak across proteins, optional symmetry prior batch leak, val/test leakage in combined_val_test reporting, stale ANM JSON values, PocketMiner baseline not run on same split, seed not applied, no seed sweep, no clean split protocol, stale claims/results, chain_id one-hot breaks PCNA homotrimer equivariance, unsafe/incomplete dataset/schema checks, and documentation drift. Task: produce an actionable project setup blueprint for phase 2 that tells us exactly how to set up the repo, data registry, split protocol, model code, training/evaluation protocol, MD validation, claims/provenance governance, and agent-based verification workflow. Emphasize how to avoid data leakage, computational errors, stale artifacts, overclaiming, and biological errors. Include required ResearchOS gates/agents and pass/fail criteria.
